clinical_coherence.notes: 66F Hispanic with CHF (EF 40%), CAD (s/p CABG), chronic atrial flutter, HTN, DM2, CKD 3b, and OSA. Presenting after syncope while standing in kitchen. HR 32 on presentation. Calcific degenerative conduction system disease as mechanism. Complex cardiac history with multiple comorbidities is coherent. Carvedilol on medication list (AV-nodal blocker requiring hold) adds management complexity.

tool_output_consistency.notes: ECG shows complete heart block with ventricular escape at 32 bpm. Holter confirms persistent CHB with pauses. EEG normal. Labs show low magnesium (1.6 mg/dL) and elevated potassium (5.2 mEq/L) requiring correction. Low SpO2 (94%) consistent with known cardiopulmonary disease. All outputs concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: R55 is acceptable. Differential correctly includes AS as contributing factor. Critical action to hold carvedilol and replete magnesium is clinically important. Electrolyte management adds a treatment dimension beyond pacing.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Straightforward is appropriate despite the complex comorbidities. The ECG finding of complete heart block is immediately diagnostic and directly explains the event.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 66F Hispanic with extensive cardiac history including CABG, CHF, and atrial flutter is realistic. Female patient adds gender diversity to the SYNC-CARD straightforward set. Medications (carvedilol, furosemide, lisinopril, spironolactone) are standard HFrEF regimen.

educational_value.notes: Good teaching case adding electrolyte management (magnesium repletion, hyperkalemia) and the need to hold AV-nodal blockers. However, the core diagnosis (CHB with syncope) is the same as S01 and S02, limiting novelty.

issues_found: Minor: R55 ICD code is symptom-level
Third straightforward case with complete heart block -- limited diagnostic diversity within the S-tier
strengths: Electrolyte management (magnesium, potassium) adds treatment complexity
Complex HFrEF background requiring careful medication management
